figures.caption: Table 1: Zero-shot task-conditioned weight generation across LIBERO benchmarks. Each block evaluates held-out distribution shift, with the NN and the MT-VLA baselines, and generates per-task LoRA adapters with WIZARD. Expert rows denote task-specific fine-tuning upper bounds.
Figure 11: Additional zero-shot qualitative rollouts on LIBERO-10.
Figure 12: Additional qualitative rollouts in Real-World.
Figure 8: Additional zero-shot qualitative rollouts on LIBERO-Object.
Figure 7: Additional zero-shot qualitative rollouts on LIBERO-Spatial.

Figure 5: Structure of the conditioning and latent spaces. The t-SNE visualizations of (left) task embeddings zi across all LIBERO suite, (middle) embeddings for LIBERO-Spatial tasks, and (right) the latent representation at the final layer of the meta-network. The plots show that task embeddings form distinct task-level clusters and are transformed into a structured representation in weight space.
Figure 4: Adaptation efficiency. (a) WIZARD matches 25-demo MT-VLA. (b) Generated weights warm-start fine-tuning and reach expert performance faster.
Figure 9: Additional zero-shot qualitative rollouts on LIBERO-Goal.
Figure 6: Additional zero-shot qualitative rollouts on LIBERO-Spatial.
Figure 13: Handheld input device used for robot teleoperation during data acquisition.
Table 8: Complete LoRA tokenization rulebook for π0.5. The total token count across all layers dictates the 5, 929 discrete tokens generated by the meta-network.
Figure 10: Additional zero-shot qualitative rollouts on LIBERO-10.
